Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: validate empty subtree chunks during state sync (M2) (#559)

* fix: validate empty subtree chunks during state sync

When a subtree receives no chunk data (num_processed_chunks == 0),
verify that the restorer's underlying merk actually has a NULL root
hash. A malicious peer could send empty data for a non-empty subtree
to silently hollow it out. This check provides defense-in-depth
alongside the H3 commit-time root hash verification.

Adds `Restorer::into_merk()` to allow inspecting the merk state when
no chunks have been processed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add sync test with empty subtree to cover validation path

加入 Embedding Search（TF-IDF + 混合搜尋）

架構決策（Gemini 分析 + Codex 設計）：
- 分層設計：預設 TF-IDF（零外部依賴），extra 可升級 fastembed
- EmbeddingProvider protocol：可插入任意 embedding 方案

- Provider state 自動持久化，跨 instance 恢復

commit.message:
Fix Flyway checksum mismatch on all DB services

Adds SPRING_FLYWAY_VALIDATE_ON_MIGRATE=false to auth, social, lists,
pins, and moderation services. The Supabase DB already has the correct
schema from the initial deployment; subsequent rebuilds produce a
different Flyway CRC32 checksum (migration files were rewritten after
first apply). Disabling validation lets Flyway skip the checksum diff
and only apply genuinely new migrations (V3+), preventing the
BeanCreationException / startup crash seen in moderation-service.

commit.message:
feat: mobile horizontal-scroll carousels (streaming-style UX)

Converts major listing sections from stacked grids into Netflix-style
swipe rows on mobile (≤768px), while desktop layout remains unchanged.

CSS changes (globals.css):
- .testimonials-grid: new CSS class (moves inline grid style to CSS)
- Mobile @media: .studio-sets-grid, .gallery-grid, .productions-types-grid,
  .bts-grid, .testimonials-grid all switch to:
    display: flex; flex-wrap: nowrap; overflow-x: auto;
    scroll-snap-type: x mandatory; -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
    scrollbar-width: none (hidden scrollbar for clean look)
- Card widths: 260px (studios/productions/testimonials), 240px (gallery),
  250px (bts) — creates peek-next-card effect on 375px screens
- .swipe-hint: gold label visible only on mobile, hidden on desktop
- .bts-card-large, .gallery-span2 grid-column overrides cancelled in flex

Component changes:
- Sets.tsx, Gallery.tsx, ProductionTypes.tsx, BehindScenes.tsx:
  added 'swipe to browse/explore ›' swipe hint above carousel
- Testimonials.tsx: grid div → className='testimonials-grid' (CSS class),
  removed inline grid styles, added swipe hint

Result: On mobile users swipe horizontally through studios, gallery items,
production types, and testimonials — far less vertical scrolling.
Desktop: grid layouts completely unchanged.

commit.message:
AOC IPC working via Trusty TIPC + kexec bootloader

AOC now fully boots and registers 60+ IPC services (audio, control,
sensors) on mainline. Key discovery: must start AOC via Trusty TIPC
to GSA HWMGR (not direct mailbox cmd 91) for proper SYSMMU setup.

- aoc_boot.c: add no_start param to skip cmd 91, use TIPC instead
- gsa_aoc_start.c: userspace tool to send AOC START via Trusty TIPC
- aoc_probe.c: diagnostic module for AOC DRAM/mailbox monitoring
- bootloader/: kexec-based bootloader (kernel+initramfs in boot.img)
  - Volume-only menu navigation, never blocks power button
  - USB networking always enabled for SSH recovery
  - Watchdog auto-reboot on hang, power long-press reboot

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add error codes and error handling (#9) (#20)

* Add error code registry and standardized error handling

- FleetAPIError base exception with 24 error codes from RFC 1 §8
- Standard error response format: code, message, suggestion, _links
- Exception handlers: FleetAPIError, HTTPException (404→ENDPOINT_NOT_FOUND),
  RequestValidationError, unhandled exceptions (500, no stack traces)
- Convenience subclasses: NotFoundError, AuthError, ConflictError,
  ValidationError, StateError, InfrastructureError
- Route matching before auth: unknown paths → 404 (not 401)
- All error responses are application/json

Implements: nexus-marbell/fleet-api#9

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix error response format and review feedback (PR #20)

BLOCKER: Flatten error response to RFC-compliant format. All error
responses now use {"error": true, "code": "...", "message": "..."}
instead of nesting under {"error": {"code": "...", ...}}. Updated
FleetAPIError.to_dict(), all middleware exception handlers, and all
tests.

MINOR fixes:
- Rename ValidationError to InputValidationError to avoid shadowing
  pydantic.ValidationError
- Add test for non-404 HTTPException path (503 Service Unavailable)
- Use "in" not "==" for Content-Type assertions in tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add TensorRT, MPS, multi-worker support, Prometheus metrics, and benchmarks

- TensorRT auto-export on first startup with file locking for multi-worker safety
- cv2 for faster JPEG decoding (replaces PIL)
- asyncio.to_thread for non-blocking inference
- NVIDIA MPS support for true GPU parallelism across workers
- Prometheus metrics endpoint (/metrics) with multiprocess support
- Benchmark script for throughput/latency testing
- Comprehensive README with performance tuning guide
- Global exception handler for cleaner error responses
- Separate run.py entrypoint for proper multiprocess metric isolation
- docker-compose.yml with model cache volume and MPS pipe mount

Benchmarked on RTX 5060 Ti: 75 RPS at 77ms P50 with 6 workers + MPS + TensorRT.

tcpdump revealed the sensor connects on port 80 (not 8005) and uses
/v1/smart_devices/{mac}/... paths (not /api/koubachi/...).

server and reachable on whatever port HA is exposed on (port 80 via
reverse proxy in a typical Docker setup). Drop the standalone aiohttp
server and SERVER_PORT entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use SmallVec for Line nodes to reduce heap allocations (#40)

* Use SmallVec for Line nodes to reduce heap allocations

Replace Vec<NodeIndex> with SmallVec<[NodeIndex; 8]> for Line::nodes.
Most lines have ≤8 nodes, so SmallVec stores them inline without heap
allocation. This eliminates heap allocations on every Line::clone() in
the merger's frequent merge/split operations.

Benchmarks show 7-10% improvement across all sizes and 43% fewer heap
allocation blocks.

commit.message:
feat: send-day scheduling with holiday deferral (#6)

## Summary

### Send-day scheduling with holiday deferral
- Add holiday reference data (`src/data/holidays.csv`) with
Zod-validated schema
- Add `checkSendDay()` service that determines if today is a report send
day in Eastern Time
- Scheduled send days that fall on holidays or weekends are deferred to
the next business day
- Gate `POST /reports/user-hours` behind the schedule check — returns
early with skip metadata when not a send day

### Unify CSV validation with Zod schemas
- Extract shared CSV row builders (`parseBool`, `parseNullableInt`,
`buildMatterCategoryRow`, `buildUserConfigRow`, `buildHolidayRow`) into
`parse-csv.ts`
- Derive `MatterCategory`, `UserConfig`, and `Holiday` types from Zod
schemas instead of manual interfaces in `types/analysis.ts`
- Move date parsing into Zod schema transforms:
- `shortDate` transform in `UserConfigRowSchema` parses `M/d/yy` →
`Date` (eliminates `parseShortDate`)
- `isoDate` transform in `ClioReportRowSchema` parses `YYYY-MM-DD` →
`Date` (eliminates `parseUSDate` and `isoToUSDate`)
- `flattenJsonReport` now validates rows through
`ClioReportRowSchema.parse()` instead of constructing weakly-typed
objects
- Rewrite `validate-data.ts` to use shared builders + `Zod.safeParse()`
for per-row validation, preserving cross-row checks (duplicate IDs,
range gaps, duplicate emails/dates)
- Add custom error messages to all Zod schemas for friendly validation
output
- Fix pre-existing bug: `topRange` schema uses `z.custom<number>()` to
accept `Infinity` (Zod 4 rejects it from `z.number()`)

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm type-check` — zero errors
- [x] `pnpm validate-data` — passes (21 users, 98 matter categories, 12
holidays)
- [x] Corrupted CSV field (bad email) produces friendly `file:row —
message` error format

---------

commit.message:
Fix 12 code review issues from PR #35: 6 critical bugs, 3 important, 3 test fixes

Critical: fix IMFSample double-Release in process_output, remove invalid
render API calls from decode thread, add Flush() after GPU Dispatch(),
make decode_queue.max_size atomic, increase UDP recv buffer to 64KB,
scope goto cpu_fallback to fix variable init crossing.

Important: remove send_tally_state() from WS callback (use invalidation),
add disconnecting_ guard to prevent WS double-close, fix port reservation
TOCTOU race with atomic insert.

Tests: add missing OBS include dirs to test CMake, fix vacuous EXPECT_GE
assertion, fix MultiSsrc test to use same timestamp for SSRC discrimination.

commit.message:
feat(loyalty): implement quests, achievements, referrals, promo codes

Four new loyalty sub-features with full CRUD, validation, and integration:

Quests/Challenges:
- Quest entity with period types (daily/weekly/monthly/special)
- User progress tracking with auto-enrollment
- Claim rewards via LoyaltyService.earnPoints()
- CRON job for expiring overdue quests (02:00 Asia/Tashkent)

Achievements/Badges:
- 13 condition types (totalOrders, streak, level, etc.)
- Hidden achievements support, 5 rarity levels
- Auto-unlock via checkAndUnlock() on events
- Seed defaults for new organizations

Referral Program:
- Unique 8-char referral codes per user
- Self-referral and double-referral prevention
- Referrer: 200pts, Referred: 100pts on first order

Promo Codes:
- 4 types: POINTS_BONUS, DISCOUNT_PERCENT, DISCOUNT_FIXED, FREE_ITEM
- Usage limits (total + per-user), date ranges, min order amount
- Pessimistic write lock for race condition safety

Includes 4 migrations, DTOs with class-validator, Swagger docs on all endpoints.

commit.message:
feat: implement do-block compilation (monadic sequencing)

Compile do-block statements (Bind, Let, Expr, Yield) into sequential
IR instructions. Bind and Let store results in variables, Expr evaluates
and discards, Yield produces the block's return value.

Also updates free variable analysis to traverse do-block statements
for correct closure capture.

Closes #26

Build SpeakUp v2: 5-screen coach app with Learn, Practice, Progress

- Rebuild architecture from 4 mode tabs to 5-screen dashboard (Dashboard, Learn, Practice, Prayer, Progress)
- Add userProgress.ts: localStorage engine for streak, learned phrases, context progress, coach plan
- Add contentLibrary.ts: reads vinh_giang.json with empty fallback, CONTEXT_TO_TOPIC mapping
- Rebuild Dashboard with streak, today's focus (CoachPlan), 6 progress rings, recent activity
- Add Learn screens: context selector + topic detail with Phrases/Vocab/Sentence Builder tabs
- Add Practice screen: merged Live (Web Speech API) + Recording (Whisper) with mode toggle
- Update Prayer: static Tips tab (no YouTube), incrementPrayerCount tracking, mode=prayer PhraseBank
- Add Progress screen: streak, rings, totals, weakest area nudge
- Add /api/plan: Claude generates 7-day coach plan from user progress
- Add /api/sentence-builder: Claude returns 5 sentence versions (Simple to Powerful)
